title: Wano Celebrates Liberation
description: On pages 12–14, this part focuses on Unknown person #10, Rayleigh, Unknown person #11, Kaido, Momonosuke, Unknown person #12 as Wano celebrates liberation while Momonosuke becomes shogun, Green Bull threatens the island, and the alliance prepares to depart.
scene: Wano celebrates liberation while Momonosuke becomes shogun, Green Bull threatens the island, and the alliance prepares to depart.

dialogue.speaker: Rayleigh
dialogue.text: Can you teach me some sword...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Hiyor!! Have you seen Luffy and the gang?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: Everyone came to say farewell this morning.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: but no one said anything to me...

dialogue.speaker: Kaido
dialogue.text: ki- Kine'mon, Luffy and the rest have...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: They said their goodbyes to denjiro and the others, why leave us out?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Just pick a different day to leave, straw hat-ya!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Our alliance has run its course! We're rivals again.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Let's at least pick different routes if we're all LEAVIN' NOW!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The islands our log poses locked onto are in three different directions. North East, East and South East.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #12
dialogue.text: North East is the most direct route.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Did you two just randomly pick the middle one? How childish.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #12
dialogue.text: Noooooo!! I lost the draw! Sorry, guys!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Get over here and help us load the cargo, Luffy!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I'm the Emperor, I should be the best at drawing lots!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You cocky emporors are an eyesore, but the one that pisses me off most...

summary: This part advances the Wano Aftermath through 46 cleaned dialogue beats, focusing on character decisions, battlefield momentum, and the story’s larger consequences.
